source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Count to and across 100, forwards and backwards, beginning with 0 or 1, or from any given number.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches stable forward and backward counting, ten-frame grouping and missing-number reasoning within 100.

objective.child_goal: I can count forwards and backwards within 100.
objective.statement: Count, order and continue number sequences within 100 from different starting points.
misconceptions: Counts objects faster than touching or seeing each item once.
Restarts at 1 instead of counting on from a given number.
Confuses decade transitions such as 29 to 30 or 39 to 40.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Counting to numbers that get bigger.
vocabulary.audio_script: Forwards means the numbers grow by one each step.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A path of stepping stones moving to larger numbers.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Counting to numbers that get smaller.
vocabulary.audio_script: Backwards means the numbers go down by one each step.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A garden path stepping back toward zero.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A full group of ten things.
vocabulary.audio_script: A ten is a full group of ten counters.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A full ten-frame with ten glowing seeds.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap each seed once as we count together.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check one-to-one correspondence before abstract number sequences.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Touch one seed for each number. One number, one seed.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Seeds glow one at a time in a small garden row.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The garden path can start anywhere. What comes after 27?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce counting on from a given number rather than restarting.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: We do not need to start at one. We can start at twenty-seven and count on.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A numbered path begins at 27 with the next stones hidden.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: After 29 comes 30. Watch the new ten-frame fill.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model the decade transition that often causes errors.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Twenty-nine has two tens and nine ones. One more makes thirty.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Nine loose seeds join a full group to become three tens.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag the missing numbers onto the path.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise forward and backward sequences with visual support.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Look at the number before and after. Which number fits?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A number path with draggable stone labels.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check counting backwards across a decade boundary.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Each step backwards takes away one.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four stepping stones dim from 43 down to 39.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip how to count on from 58 without starting at 1.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to confirm flexible starting points.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you show me where to start and what number comes next?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion waits beside a partly hidden number path.

manipulatives.purpose: Represent number order, missing numbers and decade transitions as a tactile path.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners tap, drag and speak-count stones forwards and backwards; ten-frame overlays appear at decade changes.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large touch targets, audio-first prompts, keyboard stepping and reduced-motion static reveal.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave object counting, missing-number paths and one-more or one-less only after stable forwards counting.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Practise counting on from varied starting numbers without restarting at one.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start number + 3-6 hidden path stones + ordered drag choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include plenty of starts after 20 and 50, not only 1-20.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Secure transitions such as 19 to 20, 29 to 30 and 99 to 100.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ten-frame groups + loose ones + one more or one less prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Visual regrouping must stay concrete and avoid formal column notation.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build backward counting fluency across decade boundaries.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: descending path + missing stones + distractors one or ten away
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep early items within 30 before stretching to 100.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Support audio-led retrieval for young learners and readers needing support.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spoken sequence + visual number choices + replay option
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Audio scripts need natural recorded or high-quality generated voice.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply counting to practical contexts such as planted rows, steps and collected stars.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: story context + partial count + continue or reverse task
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep reading load low and provide picture cues.

body.prompt: The path starts at 27. Put the next three numbers in order.

hints: One more fills the next ten.
question_variants.explanation: One more than 39 is 40 because 9 ones become a new ten.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts objects faster than touching or seeing each item once.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner taps fewer or more objects than the spoken count.
repair_sequence: Slow the animation to one glow per spoken number.
Ask learner to tap with the glow.
Replay the same count with one extra object.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner matches one tap to one object for two consecutive counts.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Restarts at 1 instead of counting on from a given number.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses early sequence numbers when the path begins above 20.
repair_sequence: Place a bright start marker on the given number.
Speak the start number aloud before moving.
Reveal only the next two stones and ask learner to continue.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner continues a sequence from a non-one starting point.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses decade transitions such as 29 to 30 or 39 to 40.
repair_sequence: Build the number with tens and ones.
Add or remove one counter with the ten-frame visible.
Name the new group of ten before returning to the path.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner crosses a decade boundary correctly in a new item.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use gentle garden colours, soft glows and no burst effects.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Reveal number stones instantly with static focus outlines.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read all numbers and prompts aloud with replay and slower-count controls.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use numerals, spoken prompts and picture cues rather than long written instructions.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Keep each interaction to one clear action: tap, drag or choose.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow pauses between count steps and let learners replay the sequence.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame mistakes as the path asking for a second look, with no red failure state.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach before, after, forwards, backwards, more and less with animated examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate counting on and back within 100; secure requires flexible starting points and decade transitions after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can count forwards and backwards within 100 and continue number sequences from different starting points.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Counts on and back within 100, including decade boundaries, using number paths and ten-frame representations.
next_actions: Connect one more and one less to counting paths.
Introduce comparison language after sequence order is stable.
Use quick retrieval counts in the daily warm-up.

qa.notes: Sample pack proving Year 1 maths can be audio-led, tactile and animation-rich without relying on text-heavy quizzes.
